detaileddescription.para: Zero initializes the matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0
The matrix is initialized with the contents of 'array' which must contain 16 floats. The order of the values for a transform matrix is:

@SINCE_1_0.0
matrix
A reference to the copied matrix
A reference to this

@SINCE_1_9.21
matrix
A reference to the moved matrix
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Sets this matrix to be an identity matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Sets this matrix to be an identity matrix with scale.

detaileddescription.para:
Any Matrix representing only a rotation and/or translation can be inverted using this function. It is faster and more accurate then using Invert(). @SINCE_1_0.0
result
The inverse of this matrix

Using the Matrix invert function for the specific type of matrix you are dealing with is faster, more accurate. @SINCE_1_0.0
True if successful

briefdescription.para: Returns the xAxis from a Transform matrix.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The x axis

briefdescription.para: Returns the yAxis from a Transform matrix.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The y axis

briefdescription.para: Returns the zAxis from a Transform matrix.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The z axis

This assumes the matrix is a transform matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0
axis
The values to set the axis to

This assumes the matrix is a transform matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0
axis
The values to set the axis to

This assumes the matrix is a transform matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0
axis
The values to set the axis to

This assumes the matrix is a transform matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0
The translation
inlined for performance reasons (generates less code than a function call)

briefdescription.para: Gets the x,y and z components of the translation as a Vector3.

detaileddescription.para:
This assumes the matrix is a transform matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0
The translation
inlined for performance reasons (generates less code than a function call)

briefdescription.para: Gets the x,y and z components of the scale as a Vector3. Note that transform scale always has positive components.

detaileddescription.para:
This assumes the matrix is a transform matrix. @SINCE_2_2.17
The scale

briefdescription.para: Gets the x,y components of the scale as a Vector2. Note that transform scale always has positive components.

detaileddescription.para:
This assumes the matrix is a transform matrix. @SINCE_2_4.19
The scale of xy components

This assumes the matrix is a transform matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0
translation
The translation

briefdescription.para: Sets the x,y and z components of the translation from a Vector3.

detaileddescription.para:
This assumes the matrix is a transform matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0
translation
The translation

briefdescription.para: Makes the axes of the matrix orthogonal to each other and of unit length.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used to correct floating point errors which would otherwise accumulate as operations are applied to the matrix. This function assumes the matrix is a transform matrix. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Returns the contents of the matrix as an array of 16 floats.

detaileddescription.para: The order of the values for a transform matrix is:

briefdescription.para: Returns the contents of the matrix as an array of 16 floats.

detaileddescription.para: The order of the values for a transform matrix is:
Returned Matrix = This Matrix * rhs

It makes some memory allocate & copy internally. Use Matrix::Multiply API for time critical path.
@SINCE_2_1.44
rhs
The Matrix to multiply this by
A Matrix containing the result
This Matrix *= rhs

It makes some memory allocate & copy internally.
@SINCE_2_1.46
rhs
The Matrix to multiply this by
Itself
Returned Vector = This Matrix * rhs

@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
The Vector4 to multiply this by
A Vector4 containing the result
Utilizes appropriate machine epsilon values.

@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
The Matrix to compare this to
true if the matrices are equal

Utilizes appropriate machine epsilon values. @SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
The Matrix to compare this to
true if the matrices are not equal.

briefdescription.para: Sets this matrix to contain the position, scale and rotation components.

detaileddescription.para:
Performs scale, rotation, then translation @SINCE_1_0.0
scale
Scale to apply
rotation
Rotation to apply
translation
Translation to apply

briefdescription.para: Sets this matrix to contain the inverse of the position, scale and rotation components.

detaileddescription.para:
Performs translation, then rotation, then scale. @SINCE_1_0.0
scale
Scale to apply
rotation
Rotation to apply
translation
Translation to apply

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3 &xAxis, const Vector3 &yAxis, const Vector3 &zAxis, const Vector3 &translation)
memberdef.name: SetInverseTransformComponents

briefdescription.para: Sets this matrix to contain the inverse of the orthonormal basis and position components.

detaileddescription.para:
Performs translation, then rotation. @SINCE_1_0.0
xAxis
The X axis of the basis
yAxis
The Y axis of the basis
zAxis
The Z axis of the basis
translation
Translation to apply

briefdescription.para: Gets the position, scale and rotation components from the given transform matrix.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
position
Position to set
rotation
Rotation to set - only valid if the transform matrix has not been skewed or sheared
scale
Scale to set - only valid if the transform matrix has not been skewed or sheared
This matrix must not contain skews or shears.

briefdescription.para: Function to multiply two matrices and store the result onto third.

detaileddescription.para: Use this method in time critical path as it does not require temporaries.
result = rhs * lhs

@SINCE_1_0.0
result
Result of the multiplication
lhs
Matrix, this can be same matrix as result
rhs
Matrix, this cannot be same matrix as result

briefdescription.para: Function to multiply a matrix and quaternion and store the result onto third.

detaileddescription.para:
Use this method in time critical path as it does not require temporaries. @SINCE_1_0.0
result
Result of the multiplication
lhs
Matrix, this can be same matrix as result
rhs
Quaternion

briefdescription.para: The Matrix class represents transformations and projections.

detaileddescription.para: The matrix is stored as a flat array and is Column Major, i.e. the storage order is as follows (numbers represent indices of array):

Each axis is contiguous in memory, so the x-axis corresponds to elements 0, 1, 2 and 3, the y-axis corresponds to elements 4, 5, 6, 7, the z-axis corresponds to elements 12, 13, 14 and 15, and the translation vector corresponds to elements 12, 13 and 14.
@SINCE_1_0.0
